title: Transport: bus stop
question: Where do you wait for a bus?
explanation: A bus stop is the place on the street where buses pick up and drop off passengers.

title: Transport: platform
question: Where do you wait for a train inside a station?
explanation: A platform is the raised area next to the tracks where passengers board a train.

title: Transport: gate
question: At the airport, where do you wait before boarding your plane?
explanation: The gate is the area in the airport where you wait and then board your flight.

title: Transport: tram
question: Which type of transport runs on rails through city streets?
explanation: A tram (also called a streetcar) travels on rails built into city roads.

title: Transport: ferry
question: Which type of transport carries people across water?
explanation: A ferry is a boat that carries passengers (and often cars) across rivers, lakes, or seas.

title: Transport: coach
question: What do you call a comfortable bus for long-distance travel?
explanation: A coach is a large, comfortable bus used for longer journeys between cities.

title: Transport: single or return
question: If you want to go somewhere and come back, which ticket do you buy?
explanation: A return ticket covers the journey there and back. A single ticket is one way only.

title: Transport: departure
question: What is the word for the time a train or plane leaves?
explanation: Departure means the act of leaving. 'The departure time is 10:15' means the train leaves at 10:15.

title: Transport: arrival
question: What is the word for when a train reaches its destination?
explanation: Arrival is the act of reaching a place. 'Arrival time: 14:30' means the train gets there at 14:30.

title: Travel: passport
question: What official document do you need to travel to another country?
explanation: A passport is the government-issued document that proves your identity and nationality for international travel.

title: Travel: boarding pass
question: What document do you show at the gate to board a plane?
explanation: A boarding pass shows your seat number and gate. You must show it to board the aircraft.

title: Travel: luggage
question: What is the general word for all the bags and suitcases you take on a trip?
explanation: Luggage (also called baggage) means the bags and suitcases a traveller carries.

title: Travel: suitcase
question: What large bag with wheels do most people pack their clothes in?
explanation: A suitcase is a rectangular bag with a handle and wheels, used for packing clothes when travelling.

title: Travel: carry-on
question: What do you call a small bag you can take inside the plane?
explanation: Carry-on (or hand luggage) is small enough to fit in the overhead bin or under your seat.

title: Travel: check in
question: What do you do at the airline desk before a flight to get your boarding pass?
explanation: You check in at the desk or online to confirm your seat and receive your boarding pass.

title: Travel: delay
question: If a flight leaves later than planned, what is that called?
explanation: A delay means the flight departs later than the scheduled time.

title: Travel: cancelled
question: If a flight will not depart at all, it is _____.
explanation: Cancelled means the flight has been removed completely and will not fly.

title: Travel: book
question: To reserve a hotel room or flight in advance, you _____ it.
explanation: To book means to arrange and pay for something in advance, like a room or a ticket.

title: Travel: reservation
question: Another word for a booking at a hotel or restaurant is a _____.
explanation: A reservation is an arrangement you make in advance to have a table, room, or seat kept for you.

title: Travel: destination
question: The place you are travelling to is your _____.
explanation: Your destination is the place where your journey ends, the place you are going to.

title: Directions: turn left
question: Someone says 'Turn left at the traffic lights.' What should you do?
explanation: Turn left means change direction so you are going to the left side.

title: Directions: turn right
question: 'Take the second right.' What does this mean?
correct: turn at the second road on the right
wrong1: turn right at the first road
wrong2: turn left at the second road
wrong3: go straight past every road on the right
explanation: 'Take the second right' means you count two roads on the right side and turn at the second one.

title: Directions: go straight
question: 'Go straight ahead for 200 metres.' What does this mean?
explanation: Go straight ahead means keep walking or driving forward in the same direction.

title: Directions: roundabout
question: What is a roundabout?
correct: a circular junction with a central island
wrong1: a straight road with no turns at all
wrong2: a road closed off at one end
wrong3: a painted line for pedestrians to cross
explanation: A roundabout is a circular area where roads meet and cars drive around it, usually taking one of the exits.

title: Directions: crossroads
question: What do you call the place where two roads cross each other?
explanation: A crossroads (or intersection) is the point where two roads meet and cross.

title: Directions: opposite
question: 'The bank is opposite the supermarket.' Where is the bank?
correct: across the road from the supermarket
wrong3: on the top floor of the supermarket
explanation: Opposite means directly across from something, on the other side.

title: Directions: next to
question: 'The pharmacy is next to the bakery.' Where is the pharmacy?
wrong1: across the road from the bakery
explanation: Next to means directly beside, very close on one side.

title: Directions: between
question: 'The cafe is between the bank and the bookshop.' Where is the cafe?
correct: in the middle of the bank and the bookshop
wrong1: behind both the bank and the bookshop
wrong3: on the floor above the bank
explanation: Between means in the middle of two things, with one on each side.

title: Directions: corner
question: 'There is a shop on the corner.' Where is the shop?
wrong1: in the middle of the street
wrong2: at the end of a dead end
explanation: The corner is the point where two streets join or cross each other.

title: Directions: dead end
question: What is a dead end?
correct: a road that is closed off at one end
wrong1: a very busy road in the city centre
wrong2: a road with many roundabouts on it
wrong3: a road that allows traffic in one direction
explanation: A dead end is a street with no exit at one end. You must turn around to leave.

title: Places: station
question: Where do you go to catch a train?
explanation: A train station (or railway station) is the building where trains stop to let passengers on and off.

title: Places: airport
question: Where do you go to catch a plane?
explanation: An airport is the place with runways and terminals where planes take off and land.

title: Places: harbour
question: Where do large ships and boats stop?
explanation: A harbour (or port) is a sheltered area of water where ships load, unload, and dock.

title: Places: taxi rank
question: Where do you go to find a taxi waiting for passengers?
explanation: A taxi rank (or taxi stand) is a designated place where taxis line up and wait for passengers.

title: Places: car park
question: Where do you leave your car when you arrive somewhere?
explanation: A car park (or parking lot) is an area or building where you can leave your car.

title: Places: pedestrian zone
question: What do you call an area in a city centre where cars cannot drive?
explanation: A pedestrian zone is a street or area reserved for people walking, with no car traffic allowed.

title: Tickets: season ticket
question: What ticket lets a commuter travel the same route every day for months?
explanation: A season ticket is bought in advance and covers unlimited travel on a fixed route for a set period.

title: Tickets: day pass
question: What ticket lets you travel as much as you want for one day?
explanation: A day pass allows unlimited travel on public transport for one full day.

title: Tickets: valid
question: If a ticket is valid, what does that mean?
correct: it can still be used for travel
wrong2: it has been lost by the owner
explanation: Valid means the ticket is currently acceptable for travel and has not expired.

title: Tickets: expired
question: If your ticket has expired, what does that mean?
correct: it is no longer valid for travel
wrong3: it works on every transport route
explanation: Expired means the ticket's date or time has passed and it can no longer be used.

title: Booking: confirm
question: After you book a hotel, they send you a message to _____ your reservation.
explanation: To confirm means to say officially that the booking is definite and accepted.

title: Booking: receipt
question: After you pay, what document shows how much you spent?
explanation: A receipt is a piece of paper or email that proves you paid and shows the amount.

title: Booking: timetable
question: What shows the times when buses or trains depart and arrive?
explanation: A timetable (or schedule) is a list showing departure and arrival times for transport services.

title: Travel: pack
question: Before a trip, you _____ your suitcase with clothes and other things.
explanation: To pack means to put your belongings into a bag or suitcase before a journey.

title: Travel: connection
question: If you must change from one train to another to reach your destination, you have a _____.
explanation: A connection is a second train, bus, or flight that you change to during your journey.

title: Directions: traffic lights
question: What are the red, amber, and green lights that control traffic at a junction?
explanation: Traffic lights are signals that change colour to tell drivers when to stop, wait, or go.

title: Directions: one-way street
question: What do you call a street where cars can only drive in one direction?
explanation: A one-way street allows traffic to flow in only one direction. You cannot drive the opposite way.

title: Places: terminal
question: What is the large building at an airport where passengers check in and board?
explanation: A terminal is the main building at an airport where passengers go through check-in, security, and boarding.

title: Travel: customs
question: When you arrive in a new country, which area do you pass through where officials may check your bags?
explanation: Customs is the place at a border or airport where officials check what you are bringing into the country.

title: Travel: journey
question: What word means the act of travelling from one place to another?
explanation: A journey is the process of travelling from one place to another, including the time and distance.
